added: Progression system — jobs, buildings, recipes, and upgrades now unlock gradually based on your progress. New players start with only Beg for Coins; Mining, Woodcutting, and Trade at Market appear as you gather resources. Buildings, recipes, and upgrades unlock in a similar chain, keeping the early game focused

Building consumption — buildings can now require resources to operate. Consumption is checked first each tick: if the full amount cannot be paid the building produces nothing that tick. Shacks and Houses have a small copper coin upkeep. The new Exchanger Mk I converts Copper Coins into Bronze Coins passively, but burns 1 000 copper/sec per building — it must be fed to keep running

Exchanger Mk I — a new building (unlocks after earning 10 000 copper coins) that passively produces Bronze Coins by consuming Copper Coins. Produces 0.1 bronze/sec per building but consumes 1 000 copper/sec per building. Shuts down silently when starved of copper
Statistics: Crafted Resources tab — shows balance, earned, and spent stats for all craftable goods (Planks, Cut Stone, Bricks, Furniture)
Statistics: Crafting tab — shows per-recipe completion counts and total output produced, across This Run, Previous Run, and All Time
Crafting upgrades now visible — Workshop Tools and Quality Materials appear on the Upgrades page once you have completed at least one craft
changed: Crafting queue now pre-consumes resources when adding to the queue (not when the craft starts). Removing an item from the queue refunds its inputs. This prevents resource over-spending when filling all worker slots

Job progress bar now uses the actual stored end time as its baseline instead of recalculating duration from current skills. Buying a speed skill mid-job no longer causes the bar to show 100% before the job finishes
When a job is in its final moments (0 seconds remaining) the status label changes to 'Finishing…' to indicate the game is processing completion
fixed: Manual job progress bar could visually hang at 100% if a speed skill was purchased after the job started, causing the displayed duration to disagree with the actual end time

added: Crafting system — convert raw materials into refined goods using the new Crafting page. Crafts take time to complete and consume inputs when started
Crafting queue — unlimited queue depth. Queued crafts auto-start when a workbench slot frees up

Four new craftable resources: Planks (3 wood → 1 plank, 10s), Cut Stone (3 stone → 1 cut stone, 10s), Bricks (2 stone + 10 copper → 2 bricks, 15s), Furniture (3 planks → 1 furniture, 30s). All are sellable at the Market

Craft prestige skill tree — four new permanent skills: Efficient Hands (all crafts faster), Extra Workbench (unlock additional simultaneous craft slots), Resource Saver (chance to not consume inputs), Double Batch (chance to produce double output)
Two new crafting upgrades on the Upgrades page: Workshop Tools (all crafts 20% faster per level) and Quality Materials (craft output +20% per level, rounded)
Bank rework — balances section now shows only capital currency tiers (no raw materials clutter). Added Downgrade section so you can break higher-tier coins back down to lower-tier coins
changed: Exchange rate reduced from 1,000,000,000:1 to 10,000:1 — bronze coins are now achievable as an early mid-game milestone instead of a near-impossible late-game goal
Crafting tab added to the sidebar between Upgrades and Market

added: Prestige system — once you have 10 Bronze Coins, start a New Venture. You lose all resources and buildings but earn Insight, a permanent currency. Insight earned scales with capital accumulated that venture: more bronze/silver/gold earned before prestiging means more Insight (formula: floor(log2(weighted_capital + 1)), minimum 1). The Prestige page shows a live preview of how much Insight you would earn right now
Insight weights are config-driven (prestige_config.json): bronze ×1, silver ×1000, gold ×1,000,000, platinum ×1,000,000,000 — easy to tweak without touching code
Jobs with multiple rewards now all apply correctly on completion

Skill trees — spend Insight on permanent upgrades that persist across all ventures. Three trees: Hustle (job rewards, speed, queue), Industry (building output and cost per building), Commerce (market price range, buy/sell bonuses). Trees auto-expand as new skills are added to the JSON
Job Queue — unlock via the Hustle skill tree. Queue up to 5 jobs (upgradeable to 20); queued jobs start automatically when the current one finishes
Upgrades page — in-run upgrades that boost Manual Labor rewards and Property output. Resets on Prestige. Weaker than prestige skills but available every run
Venture statistics — Statistics page now shows three columns per metric: This Venture, Previous Venture, and All Time. All Time tracks cumulative totals across every run
Multiplier system — output formula is base × upgrades × prestige_skills. Upgrades and prestige skills stack multiplicatively
Properties page and Market page now display effective rates and prices when upgrades or skills are active
changed: Manual Labor job cards now show effective reward ranges and durations after multipliers are applied
Settings page restructured with UI and Gameplay tabs; dark mode toggle moved to the UI tab
Statistics page now has tabs: Capital, Basic Resources, Manual Labor, and Properties
fixed: Manual Labor job statistics now correctly tracked per job — completing 'Beg for Coins' and 'Trade at Market' no longer inflates each other's earnings

added: Capital currency system with 5 coin tiers: Copper, Bronze, Silver, Gold, and Platinum
Bank — exchange lower-tier coins for higher-tier coins (1 billion of one tier = 1 of the next). Supports exchanging one at a time or all at once
Manual Labor — first job: Beg for Coins. Takes 5 seconds and rewards between 1 and 50 copper coins. Only one job can run at a time
Statistics page — shows your current coin balances, lifetime coins earned and spent per tier, and how many times each job has been completed
changed: Statistics tab moved to just above the Settings and About buttons in the sidebar

added: Initial release of DyWorld Inc.
Top bar showing the current system time and a menu for saving, loading, exporting, importing, and resetting your game
Sidebar with navigation tabs: Manual Labor, Market, Bank, and Statistics
Settings page with a toggle to switch between light and dark theme
About page showing developer info and this changelog
Game progress is automatically saved to your browser and restored when you return
Market and Manual Labor pages (placeholders, content coming soon)
